nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bello
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Manuel, Juan, Jorge, Mario, Roberto, Eduardo, Sergio, Alberto, Ernesto, Julio.  Portuguese Joaquim, Paulo.  Italian Antonio, Angelo, Salvatore, Carmine, Natale, Vito, Eliseo, Luciano, Sal, Aldo, Amato.

Spanish and Italian: from bello ‘handsome’ (from Late Latin bellus), hence a nickname for a handsome man or perhaps in some instances ironically for an ugly one. In medieval Italy the word was also applied as an omen or well-wishing name.
Catalan (Belló): shortened form of Abelló (see Abello ).

variants.name: Belloli
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Primo, Angelo, Marcello, Stefano.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of a pet form of   Bello  .

variants.name: Belle
meanings: Italian (Bellé): from a shortened derivative of Bello , for example Belletto.
French: from the feminine form of the nickname Bel , from Old French belle ‘beautiful’, or a habitational name from (La) Belle, the name of several places in various parts of France.
German: habitational name from a place so named in the Lippe district.

variants.name: Abello
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Carlos, Jose, Francisco, Gustavo, Alberto, Bernardo, Carols, Edgardo, Eduardo, Fortunato, Guillermo.
Catalan (Abelló): nickname from the masculine form of abella ‘bee’ (see Abella ).
Asturian-Leonese: topographic name from a Castilianized form of abeyu ‘shelter’. Compare Avello .

variants.name: Belli
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Aldo, Remo, Caesar, Dino, Guido, Antonio, Edo, Elio, Enrico, Ferdinando, Guilio.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of   Bello  .

variants.name: Bellido
meanings: Spanish: from a medieval nickname or personal name Bellitus, a variant of Bello .

variants.name: Bellot
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Benito, Manuel, Alejandra, Felipe, Jovita, Juan, Maricela, Pedro.
Catalan: from a diminutive of Belló (see Bello ).
French: nickname from a derivative of   Bel   ‘beautiful’.

variants.name: Bellone
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carlo, Gaetano, Giuseppe, Salvatore, Stefano, Vincenza.  French Baptiste, Camille.
Italian: from an augmentative of   Bello  . This surname is also found in southern France.

variants.name: Bellino
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Vito, Salvatore, Carmine, Aldo, Antonio, Constantino, Dino, Domenic, Donato, Gaetano, Geno.
Italian: from a diminutive of   Bello  .

variants.name: Bella
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Romeo, Salvatore, Aldo, Alfonso, Alfredo, Antonio, Berto, Carlo, Dario, Italia, Napoli, Sebastiano, Vita.  Spanish Miguel, Carmelita, Eleuterio, Elida, Graciela, Juan, Lazaro, Maria De La Luz.
Italian and Spanish: from the female personal name Bella, meaning ‘beautiful, lovely’ (see Bello ). Compare Di Bella .
Italian: possibly also a habitational name from Basilicata Bella, in Potenza, probably so named with Latin labellum ‘little basin’.

variants.name: Bellante
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Sal, Carmelo, Santo.
Italian:
from a derivative of the nickname   Bello  .

nameMeanings.name: Monte,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carlo, Mario, Domenic, Salvatore, Aldo, Angelo, Antonio, Arturo, Carmelo, Gaspare, Carmela, Ciro, Gasper, Philomena.  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Carlos, Edgardo, Emilia.
Italian (southern), Spanish, and Portuguese: topographic name for someone who lived on or near a mountain, Italian, Spanish, Portuguese monte (from Latin mons, genitive montis).
Italian and Jewish (from Italy): habitational name from any of the places called with Monte or Monti. Compare   Del Monte  .

variants.name: Monti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Salvatore, Aniello, Pasquale, Aldo, Carmel, Elio, Enrico, Gaetano, Marco, Marino, Philomena.
Italian: variant of   Monte  , from the plural.

variants.name: Montagna
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Sal, Gaetano, Alessandro, Antonino, Attilio, Carlo, Carmelo, Cosmo, Dino, Enrico, Oreste.

Italian: topographic name for someone who lived on or near a mountain or mountains, Italian montagna, from Late Latin montanea, originally the neuter plural of an adjective derivative of mons (see Monte 1), or a habitational name from one of the many places called with this word.

variants.name: Montini
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Enio, Pietro, Arsenio, Concetta, Dante, Dino, Domenic, Eliseo, Lorenzo, Marco, Mauro, Quirino, Ricco, Tulio.
Italian (Milan):
patronymic or plural form of a diminutive of   Monte  .

variants.name: Montes
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Jesus, Manuel, Jorge, Luis, Francisco, Miguel, Carlos, Alfredo, Ramon, Raul.  Portuguese Joaquim.

Spanish (also Montés); Portuguese (also Montês): topographic name for a mountain dweller, from the plural form of Monte ‘mountain’ or from the adjective Spanish montés, Portuguese montês ‘of the mountain’.

variants.name: Montello
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Biagio, Carmela, Marco.
Italian: from a diminutive of   Monte  .

variants.name: Del Monte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Gaspar, Angelo, Rocco, Giovanni, Pompeo, Antonio, Armelio, Berta, Camillo, Carmela, Deno, Elvio, Ezio, Gilda, Giulio.  Spanish Angel, Gonzalo, Guillermina, Jesus, Jose, Jose Manuel.
Italian, Spanish, and Jewish (from Italy): variant, with the preposition and definite article del ‘from the’, of Monte .

Jewish (Sephardic): adoption of the Spanish surname (see 1 above) at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some descendants retained the name their families used as Catholics.

variants.name: Montejo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Raul, Carlos, Eduardo, Abelardo, Alberto, Julio, Mario, Mauricio, Miguel, Miguel Angel, Alejandro, Alfonso.
Spanish:
topographic name from a diminutive of   Monte   1.

variants.name: Montesi
meanings: Italian:
habitational name for someone from a place called   Monte  , from an adjectival form of the placename.
from the plural of Montese, a habitational name from Montese in Modena province.

variants.name: Montas
meanings: Spanish and Catalan (Montás); French: derivative of Spanish monte, Catalan and Old French mont ‘mountain’ (see Monte , Mont ). This surname is most common in the Dominican Republic and Haiti.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

en.lifeSummary: Thomas de Bello Monte, Lord of Sherwill was born in 1112, in Monte, Haute-Corse, Corsica, France. He had at least 1 son with Mrs. Thomas de Bello Monte.

fr.lifeSummary: Thomas de Bello Monte, Lord of Sherwill est né en 1112, à Monte, Haute-Corse, Corse, France. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Mrs. Thomas de Bello Monte.

de.lifeSummary: Thomas de Bello Monte, Lord of Sherwill wurde 1112 in Monte, Haute-Corse, Königreich Korsika, Frankreich geboren. Er hatte mit Mrs. Thomas de Bello Monte mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Thomas de Bello Monte, Lord of Sherwill è nato il nel 1112 nel Monte, Haute-Corse, Regno di Corsica, Francia. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Mrs. Thomas de Bello Monte.
